methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $134M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Quaise Energy is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Carlos Araque and Matthew Houde that has raised $134M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers to support a robust peer-median analysis; only one of the nine sector peers meets narrow filtering criteria.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the sole applicable triangulation approach. This method applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $134M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $268M. This single-method result anchors the valuation range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $228M (low) to $308M (high), with a median of $268M. The range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties limiting conviction include the absence of granular peer comparables at matching stage and valuation tier, as well as lack of disclosed revenue, growth rates, or technology-stage milestones for the target. A peer-median cross-check will become feasible as the sector matures or as additional late-stage Climate & Energy rounds are disclosed.
